segments.title: Washington Treaty loopholes if every power cheats
segments.summary: The counterfactual tests what happens if every treaty power pushes Washington Naval Treaty loopholes in displacement, carrier, and cruiser rules.
segments.body: The discussion captures the episode's organizing scenario. Dr. Clarke starts from rule-lawyering standard displacement and fitted-for-but-not-with practices, then extends the same logic through aircraft carrier definitions, cruiser limits, sub-10,000-ton carrier ideas, and mutual knowledge that every power is exploiting loopholes. The likely result is not immediate war, but a more distrustful treaty environment in which a meaningful London Naval Treaty is unlikely and smaller cruiser-carrier concepts become an attractive way for multiple navies to stretch capability.

evidence.note: The opening defines the scenario as all powers exploiting Washington Naval Treaty loopholes and frames standard displacement as the first major gap.
The carrier and enforcement discussion moves from aircraft-carrier definitions to the absence of a court and mutual knowledge that every navy is bending rules.
The closing consequence window says a London Naval Treaty becomes unlikely, discusses sub-10,000-ton carriers, and asks whether other powers copy the cruiser-carrier route.
